conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do! I have a child, Alex, who is 17 and lives with me here in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! 

To help determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask a few more questions.

Is Alex currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex left school recently, but they've just signed up with the careers service to figure out what they want to do next.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

Can you tell me - has Alex registered for work or training with the careers service? Also, do you know the specific date when Alex turned 16, or when they left full-time education?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex has registered with the careers service. They turned 16 last year and left school a few months back, so they're still in that period where they're figuring out their next steps.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that.

Just to confirm - Alex is currently 17 years old and registered with the careers service. Can you tell me approximately how many weeks ago Alex registered with the careers service? This is important because there are specific time limits that apply to Child Benefit eligibility for young people who have left education and registered with careers services.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been a little while now - I'd say around 20 weeks ago that Alex registered with them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information.

I now have enough information to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:
- Alex is 17 years old
- Alex left full-time education a few months ago
- Alex registered with the careers service approximately 20 weeks ago
- Alex is not currently in full-time non-advanced education or approved training

Unfortunately, Child Benefit is only available for 16 and 17-year-olds in specific circumstances. If a young person has left education, Child Benefit can continue for a limited extension period if they register with the careers service or similar approved body, but this extension typically only lasts for a short period (usually up to 20 weeks from when they left education or turned 16, whichever is later).

Since Alex registered with the careers service 20 weeks ago and is not in education or approved training, the extension period would have ended, meaning you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

However, if Alex enrolls in full-time non-advanced education or approved training in the future, you may become eligible again.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Is there anything else I can help you with regarding Child Benefit or other support that might be available?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

circumstances: Child is 17 and in the 20-week extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and no longer in full-time non-advanced education or approved training. While Alex registered with the careers service approximately 20 weeks ago, the extension period for Child Benefit after leaving education (which typically lasts up to 20 weeks) has now expired. Child Benefit for 16-17 year olds requires them to be either in qualifying education/training or within the limited extension period after leaving education. Since Alex is beyond this extension period and not in qualifying education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who left education several months ago and registered with the careers service approximately 20 weeks ago. The extension period for Child Benefit after leaving education has expired, and Alex is not currently in qualifying education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.

While Alex registered with the careers service approximately 20 weeks ago, the extension period for Child Benefit after leaving education (which typically lasts up to 20 weeks) has now expired. Child Benefit for 16-17 year olds requires them to be either in qualifying education/training or within the limited extension period after leaving education. Since Alex is beyond this extension period and not in qualifying education or training, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who left education several months ago and registered with the careers service approximately 20 weeks ago. The extension period for Child Benefit after leaving education has expired, and Alex is not currently in qualifying education or training. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit at this time.
